Updated: 1/4/2001
Important news in blue



Season 5 is currently in production. Air date is currently unknown.



New DBZ on YTV (The episodes air when YTV receives them. The schedule is very fluid).



Viz will probably release future graphic novels uncensored. A press release is expected soon.



Movie #4, #5, and #6 slated for U.S. release in 2001.



Infogrames will be the publisher of the upcoming DBZ games. More information can be found in our Infogrames feature.


DVD (FUNimation):

Bardock:
The Father of Goku
(1/30/2001)

Mystical Adventure
(2/27/2001)

Android DVDs
(Feb/March)


VHS (FUNimation):

Imperfect Cell:
17's End
(2/20/2001)

Perfect Cell:
Hunt for 18
(2/20/2001)

Great Saiya Man:
Opening Ceremony
(1/23/2001)
Final Round
(1/23/2001)
Declaration
(3/20/2001)
Gohan's Secret
(3/20/2001)


Manga (Viz):

DB Part 3:
#8
(1/5/2001)
#9
(2/9/2001)
#10
(3/7/2001)

DBZ Part 4:
#2
(1/12/2001)
#3
(2/9/2001)
#4
(3/14/2001)

DB Graphic Novels:
Vol. 4
(2/9/2001)
Vol. 5
(May 2001)

DBZ Graphic Novels:
Vol. 3
(1/15/2001)
Vol. 4
(3/7/2001)

Latest Feature: Bid For Power: A Preview
Thursday - 01/18/2001

Mr.E @ 19:53 pst - *Prays to Poll Gods*
OK, if THIS doesn't work, my name is mud. New poll is on which saga you want to see on DVD first, all three choices have yet to be released on the format (Ginyu and Trunks are considered seperate sagas by FUNimation).

As to the old poll...Roughly 75% of our visitors own a DVD player. Quite impressive for new technology, I didn't expect anything above 50. Just proves that people are willing to pay for quality.

Mr.E @ 16:09 pst - More Yu Yu Info...
Wow...looks like things are starting on the right foot for once. Here's an e-mail from Gen Fukunaga via Darent Sapphie (Juunanagou.com):

1.Ā We will keep the original music.

2.Ā We will try to preserve the content of the show but usually the broadcast network dictates how far we can go.

3.Ā The release of tapes/DVD is usually driven by what our retailers tell us we need to create. They are the "800 pound gorilla" in this situation because most of a company's sales come from just a handful of major retail chains.

4.Ā We are trying to get Steve to do the translations.

Note the text in bold. I cannot believe it. Too bad the DBZ dub didn't get the same treatment. Yu Yu fans will be more satisfied than we have been, but at least it shows progress.

Mr.E @ 15:30 pst - Toriyama.org is Back...
Well, sorta. Looks like the domain thief still has their name, but the site is now available through http://toriorg.animenet.org. They've posted a brief summary of what has happened, in case you don't already know. Thanks to George of ANN for the e-mail!
Wednesday - 01/17/2001

Mr.E @ 22:34 pst - New BFP Image of the Week...
This one is of their new Northern Earth map. Looks like I'll have to download that one and get fragging soon. ^_- Check it out on their main page here.

Mr.E @ 22:26 pst - DBZ Figures in Mexico...
I'm not biased...here's a bit of international news:

American toy giant Mattel has started releasing imported Dragonball Z toys in Mexico! So far, they've imported Bandai Super Battle Collection figures, and AB and Irwin playsets!

What does this mean for US fans? Well, it will now be much easier to get some of the super-rare AB toys, such as the Mr. Satan car playset (pictured above), figures and vehicles! These toys have been popping up all over eBay, and are available in many Mexican online stores that will ship to the US. And of course, if you live close to the border, then these toys are just a road trip away.

If you asked me to sum up the quality of these figures in one word, I would respond with "shitty." If you wanted me to give a summary in no more than 1000 words, I'd say "shitty." These figures, while collector's items, are pretty damn horrible. "Buy me that useless piece of plastic!" as Dana Carvey would say. Irwin has done a much better job recently. Read the news bit and view some images of the toys here via Figures.com.

Kaio @ 19:15 pst - Dub only Indeed!
I just got through with a brief correspondance with Steve Simmons, and there's two pieces of good news. The first being that the Mystical Adventure DVD will indeed by subtitled as well as dubbed. The second is that he has agreed to allow me to interview him. I will prepare some questions in the near future and we'll set up a time. Except a full log of the interview afterwards (well, obviously).

Mr.E @ 09:10 pst - Mystical Adventure DVD...
From Anime Nation:

Funimation has announced that the third original Dragonball movie, Dragonball: Mystical Adventure, will premier on DVD in the US on February 27th, with a retail price of $24.95. No further detail on the disc has been released, but we assume that the DVD will be dubbed only. Funimation also stated in the brief announcement e-mail message, "Please note that the plan is to release one (1) DBZ DVD a month." No additional information was provided, though.

I seriously doubt the DVD will be dub only, contrary to the announcement on Anime Nation. I'll check my sources on this and get back to you guys soon.
Tuesday - 01/16/2001

Mr.E @ 23:04 pst - Better Explanation of the Animage 100...
Looks like it was taken out of context...someone from our very own board revealed how the list really works (Who, I am not sure. The article is anonymous):

I have the issue of Animage that this list appears in. I'm looking at it right now as a matter of fact. They are taking the list *WAAAAAAAY* out of context. The ranking on the list isn't an order of 1-100 per se. The titles are grouped into different categories.

The different Categories are then listed and explained:

Group 1: #1-#15 "The Blooming of Full length manga based movies to TV anime"

Group 2: #16-#29 "The Arrival of the Anime Boom"

Group 3: #30-#42 "The Rise of the Anime Fan Generation"

Group 4: #43-#47: "The World of Studio Ghibli"

Group 5: #48-#55: "The Prosperity of Comics"

Group 6: #56-#70: "The Signs of Japanimation" (this covers many titles that went to America)

Group 7: #71-#90: "The Permeatation and Diffusion of Anime"

Group 8: #91-#100: "The Readers' Picks for the Anime that should be remembered in the 21st Century"

So that's why Dragonball, Akira, and Slump were all next to each other... :P Ah well, looks like the whole community was baffled by the list...but things are straightened out now.

Mr.E @ 19:14 pst - Pop-ups from HELL...
I'm very, very sorry about the pop-ups. The internet has become a virtual deadpool, hardly any money out there for anyone. If you have been living in a cave for the past 6 months, you should read this article. In fact, read it even if you think you know what's going on. I did NOT agree to putting pop-ups on the site, and they are causing all sorts of problems (People with Netscape are having layout errors). I can't promise anything, but I'm trying to make the best of this situation. Please bear with us, thanks. :(

Update: Nuked that pop-up code, baby. Just a warning: it may return in the future if we desperately need cash for our server. As for now, enjoy the quicker load!
Monday - 01/15/2001

Mr.E @ 23:23 pst - Uncensored Manga Still Up in the Air...
Here's a quote from editor Jason Thompson in the letters section of DB Part 3 #8:

...As we explained in Dragon Ball Part 3 #2, the Dragon Ball manga is slightly edited, although not as much as the American TV version was. However, due to the many letters and petitions from fans, we are hoping to produce some sort of unedited version sometime in 2001. I'll announce it officially in this letters page when it's confirmed, until then, cross your fingers and check the Viz website for more news.

So the uncensored mangas are still in consideration, eh? Good to hear. Thanks to Jgjedi for forwarding this to us!

Mr.E @ 22:21 pst - Top 100 Anime of the Century...
Someone had to do it...since this list is so friggen huge, I've decided to just post the area of interest:

...
42. Wings of the Honnimise, Movie, 1987
43. Nausicaa of the Valley of the Wind, Movie, 1984
44. Laputa: Castle in the Sky, Movie, 1986
45. Tonari no Totoro, Movie, 1988
46. Grave of the Fireflies, Movie, 1988
47. Princess Mononoke, Movie, 1997
48. Dr. Slump, TV, 1981
49. Captain Tsubasa, TV, 1983
50. Fist of the Northstar, TV, 1984
51. Touch, TV, 1985
52. Dragonball, TV, 1986
53. Saint Seiya, TV, 1986
55. Yu Yu Hakusho, TV, 1992
56. Wicked City, OVA, 1987
57. Akira, Movie, 1988
58. Ghost in the Shell, Movie, 1995
60. Gubuster, OAV, 1988
61. Patlabor, OAV, 1988
62. Giant Robo, OAV, 1992
...

Pretty odd what anime is around Dragonball...I thought Akira, Grave of Fireflies, and Mononoke would have placed higher. Dr. Slump, another Toriyama work, placed slightly above our series. Still, to be in the middle of all these great works that come from a time-span of 100 years, it's a big accomplishment. Read the whole list over at Anime News Network right here.

Mr.E @ 17:54 pst - Bardock DVDs Shipping?
Hmmm...seems like some are getting their discs from dbzstore.com weeks in advance. I've gotten several e-mails saying that discs have been shipped. This is quite abnormal as Suncoast usually sends them out first. If you want an early copy, check out the DBZ Store.
Sunday - 01/14/2001

SkullMac @ 21:02 pst - Silly Kaio... the Kids Want Movie Clips!
Well, am I right? Just as Kaio informed me he was about to post his DVD feature, the encoding of 12 shiny new clips from the second Trunks DVD finished (and a much better encoding job if I do say so myself ;P). What a coincidence, eh? Anyhow, have fun trying to get a download spot. ^_^

Kaio @ 20:20 pst - DVD Madness!
Well, I lied. There's no madness. But there is a new addition to the feature's section, namely a DVD review! It's a fairly comprehensive exploration of the new Dragonball DVDs. Check it out if you're curious to find out more about this "digital video" that everyone (no one) is talking about!
Friday - 01/12/2001

Mr.E @ 18:59 pst - BFP Wallpaper...
Bid For Power has released a special Goku wallpaper promoting their mod. It's pretty spiffy, go check it out (And have a look at the new Final Flash that is displayed on their image of the week)!

Note: I'll be out till late Sunday Monday. Please hold off e-mails till then.

Mr.E @ 17:16 pst - Fanart Update...
50-proof goodness. Very nice job on those who made it (special thanks to Tico Rosario and W.A.R. who have sent in exceptional stuff once again). The artist in our title banner this time is HanabKu with a pretty cool Vegita scream. Check out the rest of the update here. For those who wish to submit in the future, PLEASE read over our how to submit fanart section. Thanks.

I have a few ideas of how to fix the poll...but it's going to take time. The % remains pretty stable no matter the vote count...but I'll try to fix the resetting.
Thursday - 01/11/2001

Mr.E @ 23:10 pst - Poll of DOOM...
Alright, so the last question only bemused me and a select few others...and it reset at least 5 times. This one is better, I promise!

Basically, I want to know how many of you guys own DVD players now that they've become much cheaper and more available. As a DVD otaku myself, (I now own almost 40) it's important to know. Perhaps it can encourage FUNimation to market to this new, fledging system... ^_^

SkullMac @ 18:22 pst - FAQ Fun!
Okay, so it's not exactly fun, but these FAQ updates are extremely helpful to the PN staff. Since this is the final semester of my senior year, I intend to finish on an upward note (meaning time is an even more precious commodity). In order to save more time (and knock about 20 more e-mails out of my inbox every day), I've answered the four most commonly asked message board related questions today.

Web Poll:
Which saga do you want released on DVD first?

Furiza Saga
Android Saga
Garlic Jr.

Previous Polls

Dragonball Stats

Lycos 50:
1. Dragonball
2. Britney Spears
3. Napster

Yahoo Buzz:
1. Britney Spears
2. Dragonball
3. MLK Jr.

Disclaimers
Planet Namek is just a fansite, we did NOT create Dragonball, nor did we have any part in the production process. There are some items on this website that may be questionable for those under 13 years of age. Dragonball was intended for young children in Japan who don't view such material as offensive.

©2000, Bird Studio/Shueisha, Toei Animation. Licensed by FUNimation Productions, Inc. All Rights Reserved, or as otherwise directed by FUNimation. This site is not meant to infringe on any copyrights, it is simply here to encourage people to watch the Dragonball series.

Although we did not create the series, we did take a lot of time to work on this site and bring you original files and information. Please honor our efforts by not stealing our files/layout from us. Thank you.

Web Page Design © Planet Namek

Best viewed in 800 by 600 resolution or better & thousands of colors or more.
For the best experience, set your browser to view images "better" rather than "faster."

"hey I know this is a wierd Q but are you and Meri still going out"
-- DBZ-rules
